page: Microsoft Azure E8s_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512F, AVX-512_VNNI, and AES cryptography.

This server type is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, which aligns it with memory-optimized workloads. It does not include any local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage options. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the E8s_v5 consistently in the average tier for most operations. It achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1898.68 and a multi-core score of 7757.61. Memory bandwidth is measured at 59.3 GB/sec for large-block read operations. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is relatively weak at 31.98 GB/sec, falling into the bottom 25% of tested systems. Similarly, large-scale LLM inference is highly constrained, with prompt processing for a 70B model registering at a weak 5.84 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E8s_v5 provides a high density of RAM relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-intensive applications that do not require high-performance local disk I/O or GPU acceleration. It is suited for relational databases, caching layers such as Redis, enterprise applications, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the ESv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high 8.0 GB per core memory allocation. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage. Benchmark results place the server in the middle tier for most compute and memory tasks, including a single-core Geekbench score of 1657.0 and a Redis SET performance of 7.97 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and large-scale LLM inference. This resource profile makes the E8s_v5 qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and no local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8s_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, designed for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: An Intel-powered virtual machine featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive enterprise applications and database caching.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
No bundled local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Average performance in standard compute, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations
Suited for memory-bound workloads, database caching, and enterprise applications
